summary: Israeli forces killed multiple Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon, while a US airstrike eliminated three IRGC‑backed PMU fighters in Iraq's Diyala province. Iranian Revolutionary Guard forces attacked a Singapore‑flagged vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. Shooting incidents injured civilians in Jaffa and Gaza.
key_points: Israeli units from the Golan Brigade and the 36th Division killed five Hezbollah militants in Zor A‑Sharkiya and a sixth on the Al‑Tahar ridge in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
A US Air Force strike in Diyala Province, Iraq, eliminated three IRGC‑backed PMU fighters at terror positions (Source: @shin_persian/7197).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard forces attacked a Singapore‑flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887).
Two 17‑year‑old males were wounded in a shooting on Ibn Rushd Street in Jaffa, Israel (Source: @pkpoi/142972).
An airstrike targeted the town of Beit Yahon (Source: @redlinkleb/28259).
Hezbollah militants were engaged and neutralised by Israeli forces in the Al‑Tahar ridge area (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
Violent clashes between Jolan gangs in Ghazlaniyeh, Damascus countryside, resulted in house and vehicle fires and several killings (Source: @naya_foriraq/79892).
Israel is reported to plan withdrawal from parts of south Lebanon, with the Lebanese army expected to assume control (Source: @pkpoi/142966).

sections.executive_summary: Israeli forces eliminated multiple Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon, underscoring heightened front‑line combat. A US airstrike in Diyala Province killed three IRGC‑backed PMU fighters, extending the conflict into Iraq. Iranian Revolutionary Guard units attacked a Singapore‑flagged vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, raising regional maritime tensions.

points: Iranian Revolutionary Guard forces launched an attack on a cargo ship flying the Singapore flag in the Strait of Hormuz, indicating a direct escalation of maritime hostilities (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887).

A US Air Force strike in Diyala Province, Iraq, destroyed three elements of the IRGC‑backed Popular Mobilization Units, showing Tehran‑linked militias are being targeted beyond Iran's borders (Source: @shin_persian/7197).

U.S. officials noted that lifting sanctions on Iranian oil would enable Tehran to sell oil on other markets and receive payments in dollars, though funds would be closely monitored (Source: @naya_foriraq/79882).

An Iranian source described Israel's potential withdrawal from occupied Lebanese territories as a critical red line for Tehran, reflecting strategic concerns over Israeli presence near its borders (Source: @naya_foriraq/79877).

Violent internal clashes between Jolan criminal groups in the Ghazlaniyeh area of Damascus countryside led to arson of homes and vehicles and multiple deaths, highlighting domestic instability that could affect Iranian‑aligned actors in Syria (Source: @naya_foriraq/79892).

IDF units from the Golan Brigade and the 36th Division identified and eliminated five Hezbollah militants in Zor A‑Sharkiya and a sixth on the Al‑Tahar ridge, preventing further attacks on Israeli forces (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
An Israeli airstrike hit the town of Beit Yahon, though details of damage or casualties were not provided (Source: @redlinkleb/28259).
Two 17‑year‑old males were injured in a shooting on Ibn Rushd Street in Jaffa; police are investigating the incident as a criminal act (Source: @pkpoi/142972).
A helicopter carrying President Herzog made an emergency landing at Palmachim base after a bird strike; no injuries were reported (Source: @yediotnews25/131942).
Israeli forces reported neutralising additional Hezbollah militants on the Al‑Tahar ridge, emphasizing ongoing operations to suppress cross‑border threats (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
Israel is reportedly preparing to withdraw from parts of south Lebanon, with the Lebanese army expected to assume control of those areas (Source: @pkpoi/142966).
An attack was reported in the Nasser neighborhood of Gaza, though specific details of casualties or weapons used were not disclosed (Source: @lelotsenzura/93221).
A security exercise was conducted at the West Bank crossing on Road 5, involving simulated gunfire and explosions, indicating heightened readiness in the area (Source: @New_security8200/202078).
The shooting on Ibn Rushd Street in Jaffa resulted in two teenage victims, reflecting continued civilian risk in mixed Israeli‑Palestinian locales (Source: @pkpoi/142972).
Israeli forces continue to conduct operations in the Gaza sector, as indicated by ongoing reports of attacks and security measures (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).

Israeli forces killed multiple Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon, specifically in Zor A‑Sharkiya and on the Al‑Tahar ridge, demonstrating intensified border clashes (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
A US source reported that Israel plans to withdraw from parts of south Lebanon, with the Lebanese army expected to take over those sectors (Source: @pkpoi/142966).
Hezbollah casualties were confirmed after Israeli air and ground actions, highlighting the group's vulnerability on the front line (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
A report from a Lebanese source indicated a wounded individual in southern Lebanon, underscoring the human cost of the ongoing conflict (Source: @lelotsenzura/93230).
Israeli activity against Hezbollah's southern front was described as a targeted operation to disrupt the group's capabilities (Source: @New_security8200/202086).
A US Air Force strike in Diyala Province, Iraq, eliminated three IRGC‑backed PMU fighters at terrorist positions, extending the fight against Iranian proxies into Iraq (Source: @shin_persian/7197).
Intense internal fighting between Jolan gangs in the Ghazlaniyeh area of Damascus countryside resulted in arson of homes and vehicles and several killings (Source: @naya_foriraq/79892).

Hezbollah militants were engaged and neutralised by Israeli forces in the Al‑Tahar ridge area, indicating cross‑border militant activity affecting both Syria and Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
Warplanes dropped thermal balloons over the western sector, suggesting aerial surveillance or deterrence activities in the Syrian theater (Source: @redlinkleb/28247).
No further specific Syrian military engagements were detailed in the source material.

U.S. officials indicated that lifting sanctions on Iranian oil would allow Tehran to sell oil internationally and receive payments in dollars, with strict monitoring of the funds (Source: @naya_foriraq/79882).

A statement from Oman and Iran officials noted that future arrangements for the Hormuz Strait will not include transit fees or taxes, reflecting regional diplomatic coordination (Source: @asafroz/50730).
Italy and France announced a coalition to support Lebanon after the UNIFIL mandate ends, indicating broader Gulf and European involvement in Lebanese stability (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4336).
No direct Gulf state military actions were reported in the provided messages.

key_developments: Israeli forces eliminated five Hezbollah militants in Zor A‑Sharkiya and a sixth on the Al‑Tahar ridge, curbing cross‑border attacks (Source: @idf_telegram/24445).
A US Air Force strike in Diyala Province, Iraq, killed three IRGC‑backed PMU fighters, extending anti‑Iranian operations into Iraq (Source: @shin_persian/7197).
Iranian Revolutionary Guard attacked a Singapore‑flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, raising maritime security concerns (Source: @naya_foriraq/79887).
Two teenagers were wounded in a shooting on Ibn Rushd Street in Jaffa, highlighting civilian vulnerability (Source: @pkpoi/142972).
An attack occurred in Gaza's Nasser neighborhood, though details remain limited (Source: @lelotsenzura/93221).
Israel plans to withdraw from parts of south Lebanon, with the Lebanese army expected to assume control (Source: @pkpoi/142966).
Violent clashes between Jolan gangs in Ghazlaniyeh, Damascus countryside, led to arson and multiple deaths (Source: @naya_foriraq/79892).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah remains a potent threat along Israel's southern border, as evidenced by recent Israeli killings of multiple militants. Iranian-backed militias continue to operate in Iraq, with US strikes targeting IRGC‑linked PMU elements, indicating a persistent proxy conflict. The IRGC's maritime aggression in the Strait of Hormuz demonstrates Tehran's willingness to expand hostilities beyond land fronts, threatening commercial shipping and regional trade routes.
sections.regional_response: Israel appears to be consolidating its front lines by neutralising Hezbollah cells while preparing strategic withdrawals from contested Lebanese areas, potentially shifting the balance of power. The United States is actively targeting Iranian proxies in Iraq and monitoring maritime incidents, signaling a commitment to contain Tehran's regional influence. Gulf states are cautiously adjusting economic policies toward Iran, such as waiving Hormuz transit fees, while European partners prepare to support Lebanon post‑UNIFIL.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah re‑armament activities in southern Lebanon is critical, given recent Israeli engagements. Continued surveillance of IRGC maritime operations is required to anticipate further attacks on shipping lanes. The escalation of intra‑Syrian gang violence could destabilise the Damascus periphery, affecting Iranian proxy networks. Coordination between Israeli and Lebanese security forces may evolve following the reported Israeli withdrawal, necessitating updated threat matrices.
